210.reason: [This condition incorrectly rejects valid escape sequences like '\0', '\a', '\t', etc., which have chMid values outside the ASCII printable range (32-126). The condition should also allow common escape characters.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition incorrectly rejects valid escape sequences like '\0', '\a', '\t', etc., which have chMid values outside the ASCII printable range (32-126). The condition should also allow common escape characters.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition incorrectly rejects valid escape sequences like '\0', '\n', '\t', etc. The character '\0' has ASCII value 0, which is less than 32, so it is filtered out. The condition should also allow escape sequences that are valid in C/C++ character constants.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  The condition `32 <= chMid && chMid <= 126` excludes valid escape sequences like '\0', '\n', etc., and non-printable characters. It also fails to handle multi-character escape sequences correctly (e.g., reading '\' then '0' means chMid is '\' and chR is '0', so chR != '\''). This causes valid character constants like '\0' to be skipped, leading to incorrect tokenization.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  The condition `32 <= chMid && chMid <= 126` incorrectly rejects valid escape sequences like '\0' (ASCII 0) or other non-printable characters. It also fails to handle multi-character escape sequences (e.g., '\n', '\t') correctly because it only reads one character into `chMid`. This causes the lexer to skip valid character tokens, leading to misalignment in subsequent token parsing.

25.reason: [Error: Should use token.getTokenValue() to preserve the original string representation of the integer constant, as per problem requirement. Using getNumValue() may lose leading zeros or format.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: Should use token.getTokenValue() to preserve the original string representation of the integer constant, as per problem requirement. Using getNumValue() may lose leading zeros or format.]

303.reason: [This condition will never be true because BufferedReader.readLine() strips newline characters; thus the code never reads the next line, causing an infinite loop or incorrect parsing of multi-line comments.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  [This condition will never be true because BufferedReader.readLine() strips newline characters; thus the code never reads the next line, causing an infinite loop or incorrect parsing of multi-line comments.]
